diffs.diff:
--- a/predict.py
+++ b/predict.py
@@ -0,0 +1,194 @@
+import json

+import os

+import re

+import argparse

+import torch

+from PIL import Image

+from transformers import (

+    Qwen2_5_VLForConditionalGeneration,

+    AutoProcessor,

+)

+

+def parse_coordinates(text, image_width, image_height):

+    """Parse model output to extract coordinates and convert to absolute pixels."""

+    # Try box_start/box_end format: (x1,y1),(x2,y2)

+    box_match = re.search(

+        r'<\|box_start\|>\s*\(\s*(\d+)\s*,\s*(\d+)\s*\)\s*,\s*\(\s*(\d+)\s*,\s*(\d+)\s*\)\s*<\|box_end\|>',

+        text

+    )

+    if box_match:

+        gx1, gy1, gx2, gy2 = int(box_match.group(1)), int(box_match.group(2)), int(box_match.group(3)), int(box_match.group(4))

+        x1 = gx1 / 1000 * image_width

+        y1 = gy1 / 1000 * image_height

+        x2 = gx2 / 1000 * image_width

+        y2 = gy2 / 1000 * image_height

+        return "bbox", [x1, y1, x2, y2]

+    

+    # Try point format: (x,y) single pair

+    point_match = re.search(

+        r'<\|box_start\|>\s*\(\s*(\d+)\s*,\s*(\d+)\s*\)\s*<\|box_end\|>',

+        text

+    )

+    if point_match:

+        gpx, gpy = int(point_match.group(1)), int(point_match.group(2))

+        px = gpx / 1000 * image_width

+        py = gpy / 1000 * image_height

+        return "point", [px, py]

+    

+    # Try alternative <box>x1 y1 x2 y2</box>

+    box_match2 = re.search(r'<box>\s*(\d+)\s+(\d+)\s+(\d+)\s+(\d+)\s*</box>', text)

+    if box_match2:

+        gx1, gy1, gx2, gy2 = int(box_match2.group(1)), int(box_match2.group(2)), int(box_match2.group(3)), int(box_match2.group(4))

+        x1 = gx1 / 1000 * image_width

+        y1 = gy1 / 1000 * image_height

+        x2 = gx2 / 1000 * image_width

+        y2 = gy2 / 1000 * image_height

+        return "bbox", [x1, y1, x2, y2]

+    

+    # Try <point>x y</point>

+    point_match2 = re.search(r'<point>\s*(\d+)\s+(\d+)\s*</point>', text)

+    if point_match2:

+        gpx, gpy = int(point_match2.group(1)), int(point_match2.group(2))

+        px = gpx / 1000 * image_width

+        py = gpy / 1000 * image_height

+        return "point", [px, py]

+    

+    # Try bare coordinates in parentheses

+    paren_match = re.search(r'\(\s*(\d+)\s*,\s*(\d+)\s*\)\s*,\s*\(\s*(\d+)\s*,\s*(\d+)\s*\)', text)

+    if paren_match:

+        gx1, gy1, gx2, gy2 = int(paren_match.group(1)), int(paren_match.group(2)), int(paren_match.group(3)), int(paren_match.group(4))

+        x1 = gx1 / 1000 * image_width

+        y1 = gy1 / 1000 * image_height

+        x2 = gx2 / 1000 * image_width

+        y2 = gy2 / 1000 * image_height

+        return "bbox", [x1, y1, x2, y2]

+    

+    # Try any 4 numbers

+    nums = re.findall(r'\b(\d{1,4})\b', text)

+    if len(nums) >= 4:

+        gx1, gy1, gx2, gy2 = int(nums[0]), int(nums[1]), int(nums[2]), int(nums[3])

+        if 0 <= gx1 <= 1000 and 0 <= gy1 <= 1000 and 0 <= gx2 <= 1000 and 0 <= gy2 <= 1000:

+            x1 = gx1 / 1000 * image_width

+            y1 = gy1 / 1000 * image_height

+            x2 = gx2 / 1000 * image_width

+            y2 = gy2 / 1000 * image_height

+            return "bbox", [x1, y1, x2, y2]

+    

+    # Try any 2 numbers as point

+    if len(nums) >= 2:

+        gpx, gpy = int(nums[0]), int(nums[1])

+        if 0 <= gpx <= 1000 and 0 <= gpy <= 1000:

+            px = gpx / 1000 * image_width

+            py = gpy / 1000 * image_height

+            return "point", [px, py]

+    

+    return None, None

+

+def main():

+    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()

+    parser.add_argument("--data_path", required=True)

+    parser.add_argument("--checkpoint", required=True)

+    parser.add_argument("--output", required=True)

+    args = parser.parse_args()

+    

+    # Load processor and model from checkpoint

+    processor = AutoProcessor.from_pretrained(

+        args.checkpoint,

+        min_pixels=256*28*28,

+        max_pixels=1280*28*28,

+        local_files_only=True,

+    )

+    

+    model = Qwen2_5_VLForConditionalGeneration.from_pretrained(

+        args.checkpoint,

+        torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16,

+        device_map="auto",

+        local_files_only=True,

+    )

+    model.eval()

+    

+    # Load test prompts

+    test_data = []

+    prompts_path = os.path.join(args.data_path, "test_prompts.jsonl")

+    with open(prompts_path) as f:

+        for line in f:

+            test_data.append(json.loads(line))

+    

+    predictions = []

+    

+    for idx, row in enumerate(test_data):

+        sample_id = row["id"]

+        image_path = os.path.join(args.data_path, row["image_path"])

+        instruction = row["instruction"]

+        image_width = row["image_width"]

+        image_height = row["image_height"]

+        

+        try:

+            messages = [

+                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a GUI grounding assistant. Given a screenshot and an instruction, locate the target UI element."},

+                {"role": "user", "content": [

+                    {"type": "image", "image": f"file://{os.path.abspath(image_path)}"},

+                    {"type": "text", "text": f"Locate <|object_ref_start|>{instruction}<|object_ref_end|> in the screenshot."}

+                ]},

+            ]

+            

+            text = processor.apply_chat_template(messages, tokenize=False, add_generation_prompt=True)

+            image = Image.open(image_path).convert("RGB")

+            

+            inputs = processor(

+                text=[text],

+                images=[image],

+                return_tensors="pt",

+            ).to(model.device)

+            

+            with torch.no_grad():

+                output_ids = model.generate(

+                    **inputs,

+                    max_new_tokens=128,

+                    do_sample=False,

+                )

+            

+            generated_ids = output_ids[0][inputs["input_ids"].shape[1]:]

+            output_text = processor.tokenizer.decode(generated_ids, skip_special_tokens=False)

+            

+            result_type, coords = parse_coordinates(output_text, image_width, image_height)

+            

+            if result_type == "bbox":

+                x1, y1, x2, y2 = coords

+                x1 = max(0, min(round(x1), image_width))

+                y1 = max(0, min(round(y1), image_height))

+                x2 = max(0, min(round(x2), image_width))

+                y2 = max(0, min(round(y2), image_height))

+                cx = (x1 + x2) // 2

+                cy = (y1 + y2) // 2

+                predictions.append({"id": sample_id, "bbox": [x1, y1, x2, y2], "point": [cx, cy]})

+            elif result_type == "point":

+                px, py = coords

+                px = max(0, min(round(px), image_width))

+                py = max(0, min(round(py), image_height))

+                # Also create a small bbox around the point

+                bbox_size = 14

+                x1 = max(0, px - bbox_size)

+                y1 = max(0, py - bbox_size)

+                x2 = min(image_width, px + bbox_size)

+                y2 = min(image_height, py + bbox_size)

+                predictions.append({"id": sample_id, "bbox": [x1, y1, x2, y2], "point": [px, py]})

+            else:

+                # Default: center of image

+                cx = image_width // 2

+                cy = image_height // 2

+                predictions.append({"id": sample_id, "point": [cx, cy]})

+        

+        except Exception as e:

+            # Default prediction on error

+            cx = row.get("image_width", 960) // 2

+            cy = row.get("image_height", 540) // 2

+            predictions.append({"id": sample_id, "point": [cx, cy]})

+    

+    with open(args.output, "w") as f:

+        json.dump({"predictions": predictions}, f)

+    

+    print(f"Wrote {len(predictions)} predictions to {args.output}")

+

+if __name__ == "__main__":

+    main()

--- a/train.py
+++ b/train.py
@@ -0,0 +1,237 @@
+import json

+import os

+import sys

+import torch

+import logging

+import random

+import time

+from PIL import Image

+from transformers import (

+    Qwen2_5_VLForConditionalGeneration,

+    AutoProcessor,

+)

+from peft import LoraConfig, get_peft_model

+

+logging.basicConfig(level=logging.WARNING)

+logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

+logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)

+

+MODEL_PATH = "/data/base_model/Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct"

+DATA_PATH = "/data"

+OUTPUT_DIR = "checkpoints"

+

+def load_jsonl(path):

+    data = []

+    with open(path) as f:

+        for line in f:

+            data.append(json.loads(line))

+    return data

+

+def build_target_text(row):

+    w, h = row["image_width"], row["image_height"]

+    if row.get("target_bbox"):

+        x1, y1, x2, y2 = row["target_bbox"]

+        gx1 = round(x1 / w * 1000)

+        gy1 = round(y1 / h * 1000)

+        gx2 = round(x2 / w * 1000)

+        gy2 = round(y2 / h * 1000)

+        return f"<|box_start|>({gx1},{gy1}),({gx2},{gy2})<|box_end|>"

+    elif row.get("target_point"):

+        px, py = row["target_point"]

+        gpx = round(px / w * 1000)

+        gpy = round(py / h * 1000)

+        return f"<|box_start|>({gpx},{gpy})<|box_end|>"

+    return None

+

+def process_sample(row, processor, data_path, for_training=True):

+    image_path = os.path.join(data_path, row["image_path"])

+    instruction = row["instruction"]

+    target_text = build_target_text(row) if for_training else None

+    

+    if for_training and target_text is None:

+        return None, None

+    

+    messages = [

+        {"role": "system", "content": "You are a GUI grounding assistant. Given a screenshot and an instruction, locate the target UI element."},

+        {"role": "user", "content": [

+            {"type": "image", "image": f"file://{os.path.abspath(image_path)}"},

+            {"type": "text", "text": f"Locate <|object_ref_start|>{instruction}<|object_ref_end|> in the screenshot."}

+        ]},

+    ]

+    

+    if for_training:

+        messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": target_text}]})

+        add_gen = False

+    else:

+        add_gen = True

+    

+    text = processor.apply_chat_template(messages, tokenize=False, add_generation_prompt=add_gen)

+    image = Image.open(image_path).convert("RGB")

+    

+    inputs = processor(

+        text=[text],

+        images=[image],

+        return_tensors="pt",

+    )

+    

+    return inputs, target_text

+

+def main():

+    os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

+    log_dir = f"logs/iter_{os.environ.get('RAB_ITERATION', '0')}"

+    os.makedirs(log_dir, exist_ok=True)

+    

+    # Load processor

+    logger.info("Loading processor...")

+    processor = AutoProcessor.from_pretrained(

+        MODEL_PATH,

+        min_pixels=256*28*28,

+        max_pixels=1280*28*28,

+    )

+    

+    # Load model

+    logger.info("Loading model...")

+    model = Qwen2_5_VLForConditionalGeneration.from_pretrained(

+        MODEL_PATH,

+        torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16,

+        device_map="auto",

+        local_files_only=True,

+    )

+    

+    # Apply LoRA

+    logger.info("Applying LoRA...")

+    lora_config = LoraConfig(

+        r=64,

+        lora_alpha=128,

+        target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"],

+        lora_dropout=0.05,

+        bias="none",

+        task_type="CAUSAL_LM",

+    )

+    model = get_peft_model(model, lora_config)

+    model.print_trainable_parameters()

+    

+    # Load training data

+    logger.info("Loading training data...")

+    train_data = load_jsonl(os.path.join(DATA_PATH, "unified_train.jsonl"))

+    train_data = [row for row in train_data if row.get("target_bbox") or row.get("target_point")]

+    logger.info(f"Training samples with targets: {len(train_data)}")

+    

+    # Training config

+    num_epochs = 2

+    grad_accum_steps = 8

+    max_steps = 600  # Limit steps for initial run

+    lr = 2e-4

+    

+    optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=lr, weight_decay=0.01)

+    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=max_steps)

+    

+    # Get token IDs for label masking

+    im_start_id = processor.tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids("<|im_start|>")

+    

+    model.train()

+    global_step = 0

+    total_loss = 0

+    num_loss_steps = 0

+    

+    start_time = time.time()

+    

+    for epoch in range(num_epochs):

+        random.seed(epoch + 42)

+        random.shuffle(train_data)

+        

+        for i, row in enumerate(train_data):

+            if global_step >= max_steps:

+                break

+            

+            try:

+                inputs, _ = process_sample(row, processor, DATA_PATH, for_training=True)

+                if inputs is None:

+                    continue

+                

+                inputs = {k: v.to(model.device) for k, v in inputs.items()}

+                

+                # Create labels

+                input_ids = inputs["input_ids"][0]

+                labels = input_ids.clone()

+                

+                # Find assistant response start: look for <|im_start|>assistant pattern

+                # We find the LAST <|im_start|> token which should be before assistant response

+                asst_positions = []

+                for j in range(len(input_ids)):

+                    if input_ids[j].item() == im_start_id:

+                        asst_positions.append(j)

+                

+                if len(asst_positions) >= 2:

+                    # The last <|im_start|> is before the assistant response

+                    # Mask everything up to and including "<|im_start|>assistant\n"

+                    last_asst_start = asst_positions[-1]

+                    # Find the end of "<|im_start|>assistant\n" (typically a few tokens)

+                    # Simple approach: mask up to last_asst_start + 2 (im_start + "assistant" + "\n")

+                    # More robust: find the next newline or content token

+                    mask_end = last_asst_start + 2  # <|im_start|> + assistant token

+                    labels[:mask_end] = -100

+                else:

+                    labels[:] = -100

+                

+                inputs["labels"] = labels.unsqueeze(0)

+                

+                # Forward pass

+                outputs = model(**inputs)

+                loss = outputs.loss / grad_accum_steps

+                

+                if torch.isnan(loss) or torch.isinf(loss):

+                    logger.warning(f"NaN/Inf loss at step {global_step}, skipping")

+                    optimizer.zero_grad()

+                    continue

+                

+                loss.backward()

+                total_loss += loss.item() * grad_accum_steps

+                num_loss_steps += 1

+                

+                # Gradient accumulation

+                if (i + 1) % grad_accum_steps == 0:

+                    torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), 1.0)

+                    optimizer.step()

+                    scheduler.step()

+                    optimizer.zero_grad()

+                    global_step += 1

+                    

+                    # Log

+                    if global_step % 10 == 0:

+                        avg_loss = total_loss / num_loss_steps

+                        elapsed = time.time() - start_time

+                        with open(os.path.join(log_dir, "metrics.jsonl"), "a") as f:

+                            json.dump({"step": global_step, "loss": avg_loss, "elapsed_sec": elapsed}, f)

+                            f.write("\n")

+                        logger.info(f"Step {global_step}, Loss: {avg_loss:.4f}, Time: {elapsed:.0f}s")

+                    

+                    if global_step >= max_steps:

+                        break

+            

+            except Exception as e:

+                logger.warning(f"Error at sample {i}: {e}")

+                optimizer.zero_grad()

+                continue

+        

+        if global_step >= max_steps:

+            break

+    

+    # Save LoRA adapter

+    lora_path = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, "lora_adapter")

+    model.save_pretrained(lora_path)

+    logger.info(f"Saved LoRA adapter to {lora_path}")

+    

+    # Merge and save full model

+    logger.info("Merging LoRA weights...")

+    model = model.merge_and_unload()

+    merged_path = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, "merged_model")

+    model.save_pretrained(merged_path)

+    processor.save_pretrained(merged_path)

+    logger.info(f"Saved merged model to {merged_path}")

+    

+    elapsed = time.time() - start_time

+    logger.info(f"Training complete in {elapsed:.0f}s. Final loss: {total_loss / max(num_loss_steps, 1):.4f}")

+

+if __name__ == "__main__":

+    main()
